title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week Dry Bulk: Ukraine grains to all destinations
Despite signs of recovery in the 1Q, the volume of flows is still lower than last year
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-drybulkflows/2nzhdm/321955743?h=olvQJblfjdhCN66ma6KDF7nxt2xFcQdU0D_YySc2VAk
In the closing days of May, the freight market experienced a notable slowdown in momentum, primarily observed in the Capesize segment. Conversely, the smaller vessel size segments have maintained a relatively stable outlook. However, uncertainty continues to prevail as the demand picture remains relatively weak.

children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week Dry Bulk: Ukraine grains to all destinations
children.summary:
Despite signs of recovery in the 1Q, the volume of flows is still lower than last year
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-26T110124.308.png | assets/2023-05-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-26t110124308_bb650ab8ff02.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Dry Bulk Flows https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-dynamic-drybulkflows/2nzhdm/321955743?h=olvQJblfjdhCN66ma6KDF7nxt2xFcQdU0D_YySc2VAk
In the closing days of May, the freight market experienced a notable slowdown in momentum, primarily observed in the Capesize segment. Conversely, the smaller vessel size segments have maintained a relatively stable outlook.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t)
children.summary:
Capesize - Softening | Panamax - Weaker | Supramax - Handysize - Steady
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-26T110304.822.png | assets/2023-05-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-26t110304822_9e1a8dc8dc61.png
In the fourth week of May, the performance of freight rates across different vessel size categories presented a mixed outlook. The Panamax segment experienced relatively weaker performance compared to other categories, while a steady sentiment prevailed in the Supramax segment.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - Ballasters (# vessels)
children.summary:
Capesize - Panamax - Supramax - Handysize Increasing
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-26T110411.723.png | assets/2023-05-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-26t110411723_1ff20eaa51ed.png
The number of ballasters continues to show an upward trend across larger ship categories, indicating sustained growth. Furthermore, there are signs of an increase in ballasters among Supramax and Handysize ships.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND - TonDays Softening
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-26T110557.943.png | assets/2023-05-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-26t110557943_f2ff65f15a74.png
The number of ballasters continues to show an upward trend across larger ship categories, indicating a sustained growth. Furthermore, there are signs of an increase in ballasters among Supramax and Handysize ships.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
section_path: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-26T110655.546.png | assets/2023-05-26_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-26t110655546_cef0fea5aab2.png
May witnessed a persistent increase in congestion for the third consecutive week, setting a new record high. Notably, the Capesize and Handysize segments experienced noticeable increments in congestion levels.
